observations.recommendation:
We recommended the Municipal Mayor to instruct the:

Municipal Budget Officer to reappropriate the unexpended balance of the 20% Development Fund in CYs 2015-2023 to avoid the funds being idle and evaluate the viability of the proposed development projects before these are being requested for appropriation;

Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and Municipal Engineer to:

Conduct thorough monitoring and evaluation of each approved PPA to address the factors affecting the delays in the implementation and ensure that all approved PPAs are procurement and implementation-ready to warrant the implementation of all approved PPAs for the optimal utilization of the appropriation for development projects; and

Continuously adhere to the provisions of DBM-DOF-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 1, dated November 4, 2020 to ensure that maximum attainment of desired socio-economic development and environmental management outcome of the Municipality is achieved and for the realization of the intended purpose of the 20% Development Fund.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Mayor to instruct the:

a. Municipal Budget Officer to reappropriate the unexpended balance of the 20% Development Fund in CYs 2015-2023 to avoid the funds being idle and evaluate the viability of the proposed development projects before these are being requested for appropriation;

b. Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and Municipal Engineer to:

a) Conduct thorough monitoring and evaluation of each approved PPA to address the factors affecting the delays in the implementation and ensure that all approved PPAs are procurement and implementation-ready to warrant the implementation of all approved PPAs for the optimal utilization of the appropriation for development projects; and

b) Continuously adhere to the provisions of DBM-DOF-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 1, dated November 4, 2020 to ensure that maximum attainment of desired socio-economic development and environmental management outcome of the Municipality is achieved and for the realization of the intended purpose of the 20% Development Fund.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

1. Direct the Inventory Committee to:

a. Prepare the Physical Inventory Plan and conduct the physical count
of Property, Plant, and Equipment (PPE) in accordance with the
procedures prescribed under COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated
January 31, 2020, to ensure the proper implementation of the one-time
cleansing and reconciliation of PPE account balances;

b. Ensure the timely submission of the Report on the Physical Count of
Property, Plant, and Equipment (PPE) to the Audit Team by January
31 each year, and reconcile the results of the report with the
accounting records; and

c. Prepare an Inventory Report of PPEs derecognized from the books,
and separate a report for non-existing PPE items.  Follow the
disposition procedures for non-existing/missing PPEs, and procedures
in the derecognition of non-existing/missing PPEs without available
record of accountability, as outlined in COA Circular No. 2020-006
dated January 31, 2020.

2. Require the Municipal Accountant to exert earnest effort in the
preparation and maintenance of PPE Ledger Cards, ensuring that all
records are accurate, complete, and reconciled with the property cards
and RPCPPE.

3. Require the Property Custodian to:

a. Prepare the Inventory Custodian Slip for all issued serviceable
equipment; and

b. Prepare and maintain property cards and reconcile with the PPE
Ledger Cards periodically.

We recommended the Municipal Accountant to:

a. Prepare the journal entries to record the credit memo of P150,000.00 in
the Trust Fund Regular Account (3322-1112-73), as credited by the bank
on December 17, 2024 and as confirmed by the bank as of December 31,
2024;

b. Prepare bank reconciliation statements for the four bank accounts
namely: Bottom-up Budgeting, Philhealth Capitation, Philhealth
Professional Fee, and Kalahi CIDDS;

c. Review the stale checks amounting to P16,500.00 and secure the
addresses of the said payees for replacement. Prepare the necessary
journal entries to restore to the Cash in Bank – Local Currency, Current
Account the amount of stale and cancelled checks totaling P54,354.06;

d. Ensure and examine that all of the outstanding checks reported as bank
reconciling items reflected in the Bank Reconciliation Statements do not
include stale checks and cancelled checks; and

e. Exhaust utmost effort to substantiate the abnormal debit balance of the
Cash in Local Treasury recorded in the Trust Fund account amounting
to P161,983.76.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal
Accountant to:

a. Prepare the necessary adjusting entries to reclassify the collections to be
remitted to the Bureau of Treasury, abnormal balance relative to the
fund downloaded by the National Government to the Bureau of Fire and
Protection, fund transfer from the Province of Southern Leyte, and the
collections relative to the conduct of newborn screen testing, originally
recorded under the “Due to NGA” account to the “Trust Liability”,
“Prior Period Adjustment” and “Due to LGU” account in accordance
with COA Circular 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015;

b. Liquidate and return the fund transfer amounting to P1,529,714.89 to the
appropriate Source Agencies as presented in Table 7; and

c. Exert utmost effort to verify the fund transfers without supporting
documents and send confirmation letters once the NGA will be identified
to determine if the LGU have unsettled accounts. Also, coordinate with
relevant personnel and Department Heads to verify fund transfer which
were not duly supported with documents.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to require the Agency Authorized
Officer, Alternate Agency Authorized Officer, Payroll Clerk and the ERF
Handler to and Human Resource – In-charge:

a. Reconcile with the GSIS Office the records and information of all active
employees in Calendar Year 2024 such as monthly salaries to update the
exact premium contributions to be deducted in the payroll and remitted
for the personal share and government share as well as the outstanding
loan balances to update the monthly loan amortizations to be paid by the
employee;

b. Account for the differences among the premium contributions billed by
the GSIS and the amount withheld from the payroll and the amount
remitted amounting to P209,162.78 and P658,303.83, respectively, as
presented in Tables 9 and 10, respectively;

c. Account for the difference between the loan amortizations billed by the
GSIS, the amount withheld from the payroll and the amount remitted
amounting to P43,345.85 and P7,369.73, respectively, as presented in
Tables 13 and 14;

d. Henceforth, conduct regular/periodic reconciliation of the premium
amounts and loan amortizations to be withheld and remitted with the
records of the GSIS Office to avoid under-remittance/over-remittance
premium amounts and loan amortizations. The reconciliation of the GSIS
accounts of the employees is not a daily activity and neither a year-round
activity.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant review all related LDRRMF-STF transactions for the year and prepare the necessary adjusting
entries to properly recognize the procured assets. Henceforth, all DRRM-
related procurements be properly accounted for in accordance with COA
Circular No. 2012-002, ensuring that all acquired equipment and materials
are initially recorded in the Trust Fund books, transferred to the General
Fund upon issuance, and classified under the correct asset account.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to require the:

a. Human Resource and Management Officer to inform/provide the report to the
Municipal Accountant and the Payroll Maker relative to the changes/adjustment in the salaries of the employees;

b. Municipal Accountant to ensure that the premiums and loan amortizations
remitted tallies with the amounts withheld in the payroll. Henceforth, conduct
regular/periodic reconciliation of the premium amounts to be withheld and
remitted with the Human Resource and Management Officer and the Payroll Maker;

c. Municipal Accountant/Payroll Maker to obtain regular billing for the loan
amortizations from the Pag-IBIG Office; and

d. Municipal Accountant to account for the difference between the premiums
remitted and withheld amounting to P2,838.28 (net figure) as presented in Table 16, otherwise remit the unremitted premiums, if warranted.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to require the:

a. Human Resource and Management Officer to inform/provide the report/document to the Municipal Accountant and the Payroll Maker relative to the changes/adjustment in the salaries of the employees;

b. Municipal Accountant to ensure that the premiums for the personal share
of each employee as withheld from their payroll tallies with the amount
remitted via the we access online application of the Landbank.
Henceforth, conduct regular/periodic reconciliation of the premium
amounts to be withheld and remitted with the Human Resource and
Management Officer and the Payroll Maker;

c. Municipal Accountant to account for the difference between the
premiums remitted and withheld amounting to P2,180.61 as presented in
Table 17, otherwise remit the unremitted premiums, if warranted.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to:

a. Withhold taxes at the prescribed rates for the income payments made to the
speakers and other professional fees regardless of the mode of disbursement/
payment; and

b. Include all income payments made to employees for services rendered in
addition to their regular salaries such as honoraria for BAC Members as part
of their Gross Income and be subjected for withholding tax, if applicable.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor strictly require the Revenue
Collection Clerks and Liquidating Officer to remit their collections intact to
the Municipal Treasurer and the latter to deposit intact to the Authorized
Government Depository Bank all his/her collections daily or not later than
the next banking day. Further, require the RCCs, Liquidating Officer and
the Municipal Treasurer to ensure that the prepared Report of Collections
and Deposit are duly dated and signed. This is without prejudice of the
issuance of Notice of Charge as a result of our Cash Examination.

We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal
Treasurer and Collecting Officers that the official receipts be written in hard
indelible ballpen and the duplicate and triplicate copies will be clear carbon
reproductions in all respects of whatever may have been written on the original.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor ensure that all insurable LGU
properties are covered under the General Insurance Fund (GIF) of the
Government Service Insurance System (GSIS) to protect the Municipality
against any damage or loss of its properties and the risk of non-
indemnification, in compliance with R.A. No. 656 and the guidelines outlined
in COA Circular 2018-002 dated May 31, 2018.

We also recommended the Property Custodian, as the Employee-in-Charge
to prepare and submit to the GSIS the Property Inventory Form listing all the
insurable properties and other assets, showing their latest appraised
values/valuation, appraisal date, location, and other information not later
than April 30 of each year.

We recommended the Municipal Accountant to:

a. Henceforth, refrain from restoring to Cash in Bank- LCCA account the amount of outstanding checks and the corresponding payable at the end of the year;

b. Review the stale checks amounting to P84,779.51 and secure the addresses of the payees for notification of the return of stale check in their possession for replacement.

c. Ensure and examine that all of the outstanding checks reported as bank reconciling items reflected in the Bank Reconciliation Statements do not include cancelled checks and stale checks.

c. Ensure and examine that all of the outstanding checks reported as bank reconciling items reflected in the Bank Reconciliation Statements do not include cancelled checks and stale checks.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to direct the Municipal Planning and development Coordinator that in preparation of the Annual Investment Plan, conduct a thorough review of the Peace and Order and Public Safety (POPS) Plan (POPS) Plan of the LGU and ensure that all proposed PPAs are aligned with the priorities outlined in the POPS Plan.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal Accountant to: a. Reconcile the difference in balances among the bank confirmation replies, the bank reconciliation statements and record the book reconciling items, if warranted; and b. Prepare a Journal Voucher at the end of the year based on the list of unreleased check submitted by the Municipal Treasurer’s Office. At the start of ensuing year, draw another entry to reverse the previous entry made.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal Accountant to: a. Exert earnest effort to verify and identify the Source Agencies of the fund transfer tabulated in Table 4. Once identified, send confirmation letters to the National Government Agencies concerned to determine if the Municipality has unsettled accounts; b. Send confirmation letters to all government agencies listed in Table 5 and exert earnest effort to obtain copy of Memorandum of Agreement or its equivalent document; c. Reclassify the erroneous recognition of Due to NGA account relative to the fund transfer from the Province of Southern Leyte amounting to P30,000.00 and henceforth, comply with COA Circular 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015 which prescribes the Revised Chart of Accounts for Local Government Units; d. Verify as to when the fund transfer balances initially existed, particularly the old accounts aggregating P2,282,112.70, to determine if it is already dormant and request for its write off following COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016; e. Monitor the status of utilization of fund transfer enumerated in Table 7 and liquidate the fund transfer listed in Table 8 whose purpose was already completed as required in COA Circular 94-013 dated December 13, 1994; and f. Henceforth, prepare regularly the Aging of Fund Transfer on a quarterly basis and conduct regular and periodic verification, analysis, and validation of the existence of the fund transfer as required by COA Circular 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor and Municipal officials concerned to: a. Ensure that all approved PPAs are procurement and implementation-ready and warrant the implementation of all approved PPAs for the optimal utilization of the appropriation for development projects; b. Verify if the implementation of the prior year PPAs are still feasible and/or implementable, otherwise, consider the said projects to be reprogrammed; and c. Continuously adhere to the provisions of DBM-DOF-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 1, dated November 4, 2020 to ensure that maximum attainment of desired socio-economic development and environmental management outcome of the Municipality is achieved and for the realization of the intended purpose of the 20% Development Fund.

observations.recommendation:
1.19 We recommended the Municipal Mayor to:
1. Instruct the Inventory Committee to:
a. Prepare and submit a complete Report on the Physical Count of
Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) to the Audit Team.
Henceforth, submit the RPCPPE not later than January 31 of each
year;
b. Implement the one-time cleansing of PPE and conduct the physical
count pursuant to COA Circular No. 2020-006;
2. Require the Property Custodian-Designate to:
a. Properly fill out and prepare property cards for each type of PPE;
b. Promptly record and update property cards for any receipt and
disposition of PPE, and reconcile with the PPE Ledger Cards and
RPCPPE;
c. Prepare the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable
Property for all PPEs found unserviceable; and
3. Require the Municipal Accountant-Designate to exert earnest effort in
the preparation and maintenance of PPE ledger cards and reconcile with
the property cards and RPCPPE.

We recommended the Municipal Accountant to:
a. Henceforth, refrain from restoring to Cash in Bank -LCCA account the
amount of outstanding checks and the corresponding payable at the end
of the year;
b. Review the stale checks amounting to P84,779.51 and secure the
addresses of the payees for notification of the return of stale check in
their possession for replacement.
c. Ensure and examine that all of the outstanding checks reported as bank
reconciling items reflected in the Bank Reconciliation Statements do not
include cancelled checks and stale checks.

3.12 We recommended the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal
Accountant to:
a. Prioritize the liquidation of the fund transfer amounting to P8,882,241.68
and return the unused/unutilized fund, if any, to the corresponding
Source Agencies;
b. Closely monitor the status of utilization of fund transfer enumerated in
Table 8 and cause the liquidation of the fund transfer amounting to
P3,536,666.73 and return the unused/unutilized fund, if any, to the Source
Agencies;
c. Reclassify the erroneous recognition of Due to NGA account relative to
the fund transfer from the Province of Southern Leyte and Department
of Health amounting to P30,000.00 and P12,850.00, respectively, and
henceforth, comply with COA Circular 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015
which prescribes the Revised Chart of Accounts for Local Government
Units;
d. Exert earnest effort to identify the nature of the fund transfers credited to
the account of the Municipality and the Sources thereof on a timely basis
for the fund transfer tabulated in Table 8. Once identified, send
confirmation letters to the National Government Agencies concerned to
determine if the Municipality has unsettled accounts; and
e. Henceforth, prepare regularly the Aging of Fund Transfer on a quarterly
basis and conduct regular and periodic verification, analysis, and
validation of the existence of the fund transfer as required by COA
Circular 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

4.14 We recommended the Municipal Mayor, through the Officer-In-Charge of
the RHU, to:
1. Issue a Special Order covering the assignment of public health workers to
hazardous/difficult areas;
2. Secure Certification by the Secretary of the DOST/DOH/DND/Director of
the PHILVOCS that the place of assignment/travel is a strife-
torn/embattled/disease-infested/distress or isolated areas/stations, or
areas declared under state of calamity or emergency, or with volcano
activity or eruption; and
3. Attach the complete supporting documents to the disbursement vouchers.
We recommended the Municipal Accountant to:
1. Submit the lacking Daily Time Records of the PHWs to facilitate the
computation of the overpayment of benefits; and
2. Process disbursement vouchers only when they are supported with
complete documentation, and adhere strictly to the guidelines on the
grant of hazard pay, subsistence, and laundry allowance.
We recommended the Public Health Workers presented in Table 12 to
refund the amount equivalent to the overpayment.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the OIC-Supply
Officer to:
a. Use the required format of a sequentially numbered Driver’s Trip Ticket
duly approved by the Agency Head or the authorized representative with
complete information being filled out for all travels undertaken;
b. Reconcile every month the Driver’s Trip Ticket with the Monthly Fuel
Consumption Report and Summary of Monthly Report of Official Travels as
mandated under COA Circular No. 77-61 to determine whether the said fuel
usage is valid and used appropriately;
c. Utilize the prescribed form such as the Requisition and Issue Voucher,
Summary of Requisition and Issue Voucher and Daily Gas Issue Record as a
tool in the monitoring of the receipt and issuance of the fuel;
d. Enforce the “No Properly filled-up Driver’s Trip Ticket, No Trip Policy”;
e. As to the distance travelled, to determine the accurate consumption of fuel:
- All vehicles with functional odometer should record the kilometer reading
before and after the trip
- All vehicles with non-functional/defective odometer should indicate the
approximate distance traveled including the number of trips undertaken
during the day. Immediate repair of the odometer should be considered.
f. Submit to the Audit Team the list of vehicles which consumed the 3,850.20
liters of fuel, oil, and lubricants, Approved Driver’s Trip Tickets, the
Monthly Report of Official Travels and Monthly Report of Fuel
Consumption.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to direct the Municipal Planning
and Development Coordinator that in preparation of the Annual Investment
Plan, conduct a thorough review of the Peace and Order and Public Safety
Plan (POPS) Plan of the LGU and ensure that all proposed PPAs are aligned
with the priorities outlined in the POPS Plan.

We likewise recommended that the Municipal Mayor, as Chairman of the
Local Peace and Order Council to include only those PPAs that are directly
related to crime and disorder, illegal drugs, conflict, threats to the
environment and human security, and other related-peace and order
concerns that may be deemed necessary.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to direct the Municipal Accountant
to transfer all unexpended balances of the QRF and DRRMF-MOOE for CY
2023 to the Special Trust Fund. Henceforth, ensure that all unexpended
balances of the LDRRMF shall be transferred to the Special Trust Fund at
the end of each year.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to Direct the LDRRM Officer,
together with the MDRRM Council to:
a. Timely implement all LDRRM related PPAs to achieve the goals and
objective of the fund as well as the desired benefits; and
b. Stop the use of LDRRMF for non-disaster related activities and strictly
follow the guidelines in the utilization of the LDRRMF as provided under
Section 21 of R.A. No. 10121. This is without prejudice to the issuance of
Notice of Suspension/Disallowance.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to instruct the:
1. Municipal Budget Officer to reappropriate the unexpended balance of the
20% Development Fund in CYs 2015-2023 to avoid the funds being idle and
evaluate the viability of the proposed development projects before these are
being requested for appropriation;
2. Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and Municipal
Engineer to:
a. Conduct thorough monitoring and evaluation of each approved PPA to
address the factors affecting the delays in the implementation and
ensure that all approved PPAs are procurement and implementation-
ready to warrant the implementation of all approved PPAs for the
optimal utilization of the appropriation for development projects; and
b. Continuously adhere to the provisions of DBM-DOF-DILG Joint
Memorandum Circular No. 1, dated November 4, 2020 to ensure that
maximum attainment of desired socio-economic development and
environmental management outcome of the Municipality is achieved
and for the realization of the intended purpose of the 20% Development
Fund.

10.16 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, through the BAC Chairperson
to:
a. Submit justification why the contractor was awarded with new contracts
despite the non-disclosure of the percentage of actual accomplishment
vis-à-vis percentage of planned accomplishment of its on-going projects
and despite the inconsistent and incomplete disclosures of its ongoing
projects; and
b. Require submission of the latest Business Tax Returns within five calendar
days from receipt by the bidder of the notice from the BAC that the bidder
has the Lowest Calculated Bid or Highest Rated Bid.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to: 1. Instruct the Inventory Committee to prepare and submit a complete Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) to the Audit Team. Henceforth, submit the RPCPPE not later than January 31 of each year; 2. Require the Property Custodian to: a. Properly fill out and prepare property cards for each type of PPE; b. Promptly record and update its property cards for any receipt and disposition of PPE, and reconcile with the PPE Ledger Cards periodically; c. Prepare the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property for all PPEs found unserviceable; and 3. Require the Municipal Accountant to: a. Act on the preparation and maintenance of the PPE ledger cards based on the source documents of the transactions; and b. Provide the necessary disclosures required in IPSAS 17 on PPE.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor to require the: a. Municipal Treasurer and Collecting Officers that the original copy of the official receipts be written in hard indelible ballpen and the duplicate and triplicate copies will be the duplicate and triplicate copies to be submitted to the Audit Team should be carbon reproductions of the original and not photocopies of the original and/or the triplicate; and b. Henceforth, prioritize the safekeeping of the official receipts as required in Section 101 of PD 1445.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to require the: a. Refrain from designating and/or assigning the Job Order Personnel to perform the duties of the regular employees such as the collection of taxes, fees, among others; b. Require all the Revenue Collection Clerks to remit to the Municipal Treasurer the full amount of their collections on a daily basis as required by COA Circular No. 92-382; c. Ensure that the dates of remittance of collections by the Revenue Collection Clerks and the receipt thereof by the Municipal Treasurer be indicated and/ or identified in the Report of Collections and Deposit, or in any equivalent document for monitoring purposes; and d. Direct the Municipal Treasurer to indicate the date of receipt of collections and remittances from the Revenue Collection Clerks and ensure that the deposit of the collections to the Authorized Government Depository Banks are made daily or on the next banking day and/or if the collections reach P10,000.00 as required in the Item 7, Chapter II of COA Memorandum 2013-004 dated July 9, 2013 or the Revised Cash Examination Manual.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to direct: 1. Municipal Accountant to record the full amount of RPT due and collectible as receivables based on the computed amount per RPT tax account register/taxpayer’s index cards; 2. Municipal Assessor to: a. Update and reconcile records relative to real properties within the Municipality and its owners; b. Appraise and revise the real property assessment every three year as required in Section 219 of RA 7160; and c. Conduct physical surveys to verify and determine whether all real properties within the Municipality are properly listed in the assessment rolls. 3. Municipal Treasurer to: a. Reconcile the updated tax payer’s index card with the records of the Municipal Assessor; b. Submit the Tax payer’s index card to the Municipal Accountant at the start of year as basis in recording RPT receivables; and c. Develop collection strategies and intensify the collection of real property taxes and special education taxes to provide additional sources of funds of the Municipality.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to require the Local Chief Executive and the concerned officials to: a. ensure proper handling and storage of vaccines and adopt monitoring strategies and procedural controls to minimize vaccine wastage; and b. adopt recordkeeping and reporting measures to account for vaccine receipts, issuance and utilization in accordance with the DOH guidelines.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the
Municipal Mayor require the:
1. Municipal Accountant to maintain subsidiary
ledger card for each of group of PPEs;
2. Property custodian to record promptly each transaction and update its property cards as prescribed in the GAM for LGUs; and
3. Inventory Committee to prepare RPCPPE with complete data for each item, as basis for reconciliation with the SLCs and PCs. Also, furnish the Audit Team a copy of the duly accomplished RPCPPE not later than January 31 of each year in accordance with the GAM for LGUs.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to prepare Property and Inventory Committee guidelines prior to the inventory taking with clear instructions that all tangible assets are to be counted and define roles of the members of the Inventory Committee to ensure the timely completion and submission of the RPCPPE.
We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to direct the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary disclosure on the dormant balances of the Due from NGOs and other LGUs account.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to direct the Municipal Accountant to request for the write off of the dormant accounts with validation for the identified the dead debtors, no pending cases, with unknown whereabouts and have exhausted all possible means for the collection.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to direct the Municipal Engineer and the Bids and Awards Committee to implement immediately the projects under the 20% Development Fund and to prioritize the conduct of bidding over other activities pursuant to pertinent provisions of RIRR of RA No. 9184.
We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to direct the LDRRMO to implement programs of LDRRMP that are being monitored in order to efficiently respond to calamity thereby ensuring sustained recovery.
We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to direct the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary disclosure with the dormant balances of the account.
We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal Health Officer facilitate the licensing of the ambulance vehicles.
We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to direct the Municipal Accountant enforce stringent measures for the settlement of disallowances.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the GAD Focal Person to train personnel assigned to man VAW Help Desks to capacitate them to man VAW Help Desks to address cases on women violations in gender-responsive manner.
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the GAD Focal Person to establish a GAD Monitoring and Evaluation System.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor enjoin the Municipal Accountant and
General Services Officer - Designate to identify the discrepancies between the
financial statement balances and RPCPPE and the unknown PPE totalling
P14,319,624.73. Likewise, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor consider the
adoption of the guidelines and procedures on the one – time cleansing of PPE
prescribed by COA Circular No. 2020 – 006.

Also, after the conduct of inventory taking for one-time cleansing of PPE, we
recommended to establish the existence and condition of the PPE without
accumulated depreciation and if necessary, prepare adjusting entries to reflect
the correct net book value of PPE as of the reporting date and to provide their
depreciation for the ensuing period.

Lastly, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Budget
Officer to provide budgetary requirements to cover the necessary expenses in the
conduct of inventory taking.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. Municipal Accountant to exhaust all necessary measures to retrieve the
related documents to determine the accountabilities associated with the
Breeding stocks;
b. General Services Officer to determine the existence of the Breeding
Stocks by conducting a comprehensive inventory of the Biological
Assets recorded in the books;
c. General Services Officer and the Municipal Accountant to prepare the
required ledger and property stock cards, upon determination of the assets’
existence;
d. Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the General Services Officer to
reconcile the books of accounts with the results of physical inventory; and
e. General Services Officer and the Municipal Accountant to ensure that every
Biological Assets are supported with the required ledger and property
stock cards.

We reiterated our previous year recommendation that the Municipal Mayor
instruct the Municipal Treasurer effect the cancellation of the stale checks and
furnish the Municipal Accountant with the original DV and its supporting
documents for the preparation of JEV in the municipality’s books of accounts to
fairly present its financial statements as of December 31, 2021.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to
reconcile with the Municipal Treasurer the P12,041.73 unreconciled balance
between the GL and the Cashbook. Likewise, the Municipal Treasurer and the
Municipal Accountant to recompute the correct balance of Cash Book and Cash in
Bank – GF as reflected in the bank reconciliation statement, respectively and
prepare the necessary adjusting entries to reflect the correct balance of the Cash
accounts and fairly present municipality’s financial statements as of December 31,
2021.

With these, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Bids and
Awards Committee (BAC) and Technical Working Group (TWG):
a. to scrutinize the sufficiency of the winning bidder’s manpower on all the
projects to be awarded and not on an individual basis only in cases where
the latter is the lowest bidder in several projects which have similar or
overlapping implementation period in order to oblige the winning
contractor to supplement its manpower to be utilized in the projects
awarded to it or to disqualify it from obtaining several awards or contracts
in conformity with the provisions of Section 34.1 and 34.3 of the Revised
IRR of RA No. 9184;
b. to monitor contractors with on-going projects with other government
agencies and to coordinate with them involved in implementing
infrastructure projects not disclosed in the Statement of On-going and
Completed projects to avoid overlapping and delay in project
accomplishment and/or substandard quality of work; and
c. to ensure that pertinent provisions of Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 are
strictly adhered to promote transparency and economy of its procurement
process.

We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Engineer
to monitor strictly and closely all projects simultaneously implemented by the
contractors and prepare an individual status report per project to be submitted to
the Honorable Mayor for monitoring purposes and in order to check, verify and
evaluate the quality of work and efficiency of the said contractors.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to
coordinate with the responsible officials to facilitate the immediate submission of
the lacking documents. Henceforth, ensure that all documentary requirements for
emergency purchase are complied with before certifying as to completeness of
the supporting documents of the DVs.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Assessor and
Municipal Accountant to submit lacking documentary requirements to support the
payment of the purchased land as prescribed under PD No. 144 and COA Circular
No. 2012-001 for further evaluation of the Audit Team.

In view of this, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct:
a. the Municipal Engineer to:

Ensure that grants of suspension of work are compliant with the Sections 9 of
Annex E of the 2016 IRR of RA 9184; and
b. all concerned officials and employees to work hand in hand for the prompt
completion and implementation of the planned projects.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor instruct the Bids and Awards Committee
(BAC) Chairman to refrain from resorting to procurement and disbursement
through reimbursement basis. All payments should be made directly to the
supplier/contractor by check in order for the Municipality to deduct and withhold
applicable taxes and remit the same in accordance with the BIR regulations.
Ensure that all disbursements will be supported with complete documentation.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC) to ensure the
timely, effective and efficient implementation of the approved priority
development projects within target completion period;
b. Municipal Development Council (MDC) to conduct in-depth planning for
prioritization of Projects, Programs and Activities (PPAs) in the 20 percent
Development Fund by the consensus of the constituents and in
coordination with the MPDC to avoid realignment and sudden change of
projects intended for current year’s implementation; and
c. Municipal Budget Officer, Municipal Engineer and Municipal Accountant
ensure that all expenditures shall be supported by proper appropriations
and that funds shall only be available exclusively for the specific purpose
for which they have been appropriated.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Local Disaster Risk
Reduction and Management Officer (LDRRMO) and the Municipal Budget Officer
to ensure that changes in the annual budget for the disaster fund be made in
accordance with Article 417 and 454 (b) of the IRR of RA No. 7160.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the honorable members of the
Sanggunian include among its top priorities the construction of a building for the
safekeeping and accommodation of the increasing volume of documents
generated by the various departments/offices of the Municipality, particularly
those for submissions to the audit team inconsideration of the preservation
period of ten (10) years as mandated by law.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require all concerned
Department Heads/employees to submit detailed action plans on the CY 2020 AAR
findings and recommendations, including that of prior years, to be incorporated in
the AAPSI, and to submit the AAPSI to the Audit Team. Likewise, assign a focal
person who will prepare the AAPSI and monitor the compliance of each
concerned Department Head/employee regarding the submission of detailed
action plans.

Moreover, we recommended that the management follow strictly the above
provisions of the GAA and COA Memorandum No. 2014-002 on the submission of
the AAPSI within 60 days from the receipt of the COA Annual Audit report to give
enough time for the Auditor to review, monitor and evaluate the action taken by
the management on the audit recommendations.

1.7 We recommended that:
1. The Municipal Treasurer to prepare at year end a List of Unreleased
Checks to be attached to the Report of Checks Issued and submit to
the Municipal Accountant and to COA Auditor;
2. The Municipal Accountant should restore unreleased checks by
preparing a journal entry voucher at the end of the year based on the list submitted from the MTO. At the start of the ensuing year, draw another entry to reverse the previous entry made and recognize the availability of the checks for release;
3. The Municipal Accountant to review the long outstanding/stale checks
and secure the addresses of the payees for notification of the return of stale checks in their possession for replacement; and

2.6 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
1. Locate all source documents to review the terms and conditions
corresponding to the funds transferred in relation to the
implementation of programs and projects;
2. Determine if the specific programs or projects for which the funds
were transferred were implemented, goods/services were delivered,
liquidation of funds were already submitted and received by the
source agencies, and the liabilities were settled for proper adjustments
in the books;
3. Return to the source agency any unused/unexpended balance; and
4. Request authority for the write off of dormant fund transfer accounts
in accordance with COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19,
2016.

3.6 We recommended for the Municipal Mayor to direct the Chief of the BAC Secretariat
to comply with the minimum documentary requirements for the procurement of
infrastructure projects as prescribed under the provisions of Presidential Decree No.
1445, RA 9184 and its IRR including the applicable guidelines set forth under items
9.1.1 and 9.1.1.4 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012, 9184 and to
exercise due diligence in the processing of claims to ensure complete documentation.

3.7 We further recommended that the documents be submitted as soon as
possible for proper disposition in audit.

4.9 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal
Accountant:
a) to send within ten (10) days before the expiration of the prescribed period a
written demand signed by the Municipal Mayor or his duly authorized
representative requiring officials and employees listed on the table above
to settle their cash advance immediately because failure to liquidate the cash
advances is presumed that funds have been used for personal purposes;
b) to cause the immediate withholding of salaries and claims of officers and
employees who failed to settle their cash advances within the prescribed
period after being sent notice and demand; and
c) to write-off cash advances of deceased Accountable Officers.

5.11 In order to establish the regularity of the grant of the AHDP/SRA, we
recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant
to immediately submit the lacking supporting documents for the proper
disposition in audit of the disbursement. Henceforth, only disbursement
vouchers/payrolls with complete supporting documents should be processed
for payment.

6.9 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. As Chairman of the MDRRM Council, together with the Members
thereof, prepare the LDRRMFIP that would encompass PPAs to be
charged to the Current Year, Continuing Appropriations, and Prior
Year’s Unexpended Balance under the Special Trust Fund and
Incorporated in the Annual Investment Plan and which will serve as basis
for the preparation of the Annual and Supplemental Budget of the
Municipality; and
b. Direct the Municipal Accountant to revert to the unappropriated surplus
of the General Fund, the amount of P536,120.29 unutilized balance for
CY 2016 in the Special Trust Fund which remained unused for five years.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. create an Inventory Committee for the conduct of complete and detailed physical count of all the Municipality’s properties taking into account the provisions mandated under COA Circular 2020-006; b. instruct the Inventory Committee to properly utilize the required inventory reports stated on the above-mentioned Circular; c. order the Office of the GSO to prepare property Acknowledgement Receipt for all PPEs; d. require the Municipal Accountant to prepare the required ledger cards for all properties.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality created the Inventory and Appraisal and Disposal Committee by virtue of Administrative Order No. 012, series of 2020 on June 25, 2020. However, the duties and functions of the created Inventory Committee are pertaining only to facilitation of assessment, inventory appraisal and disposal of assets and not to implement the one-time cleansing of PPE as mandated under COA Circular 2020-006.
observations.reason: The duties and functions of the created Inventory Committee are pertaining only to facilitation of assessment, inventory appraisal and disposal of assets and not to implement the one-time cleansing of PPE as mandated under COA Circular 2020-006.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
• The Municipal GSO shall conduct the physical count of inventories every semester and prepare the Report of Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI). Submit copy of RPCI to the Municipal Accountant and to the Audit Team within the prescribed period; and
• The Municipal Accountant to reconcile with the submitted RPCI of the GSO with the records of accounting. Also, apply consistently the perpetual inventory accounting method on all supplies and materials to fairly present the inventory and expense accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: As per GSO Designate, they were conducting inventory of common-used supplies prior to purchase of new sets of these supplies.

observations.recommendation: We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Social Welfare and Development to submit an explanation letter for the 162 ineligible beneficiaries that were given subsidies.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Social Welfare and Development Officer explained that some of 162 families who received financial assistance, were not aware that they will be beneficiaries of other agencies and while others have family members working in the company thus received financial assistance from DOLE. The said 162 families were no longer included in DSWD SAP for the 2nd tranche. Likewise, IDs with specimen of signatures of the beneficiaries, SAC forms and some pictures on the actual distribution of SAP to beneficiaries were not attached in the liquidation contrary to the same guidelines.
observations.reason: Lacking photos and pictures not yet submitted to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor to instruct the concerned officials and employees to:
 a. immediately submit the lacking documents to validate propriety and reasonableness of the procurement made by the Municipality; and
 b. strictly comply with the provisions of GPPB Circular No. 01-2020 dated April 06, 2020 and IRR of RA No. 9184. We also recommended that the Municipal accountant to provide justification on the noted deficiencies in the liquidation of cash advance and reconcile with it all the necessary liquidation documents to prove its full liquidation.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant agreed to comply with the auditor’s recommendations.
observations.reason: Lacking documents not yet submitted to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to create a special account in the general fund for the Bayanihan Grant to Cities and Municipalities for easy monitoring/tracking of the utilization of the allocation, pursuant to Section 3.7 of Local Budget Circular No. 125.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant monitored the BGCM balance by recording it in the Trust Account in the General Fund and supported with subsidiary ledgers for control.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the head in charge of service vehicles to require trip tickets for all travels of every service vehicle before the service trip and that drivers conduct the refilling of gas tanks for every travel so that the amount of used fuel for every trip could be properly monitored. We also recommended the Municipal Budget Officer to include in the budget the amount of repair of all defective odometers to completely monitor the distance travelled per trip for all service vehicles. We also recommended the Municipal Accountant to provide justification why the incomplete documents on fuel consumed (Driver’s Trip Tickets) were not being checked by their office.
observations.managementAction: As per Municipal Accountant, the Accounting Office also records outright expenses the purchase of various inventories. The Municipality included Repairs and Maintenance of government in the appropriations. The Municipal Accountant explained that to expedite the payment balances to the Gasoline station which is required before refilling fuel to Municipal vehicles when credit limit was exceeded; they rely on the promise of the employees concerned that they will provide the necessary documents.
observations.reason: Samples trip tickets were not properly filled up and no signatures from authorized personnel. Lack of Manpower who will monitor this audit observation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC) to ensure the efficient and effective utilization of the 20% Development Fund; and that changes in the Annual Budget be implemented in accordance with Article 417 and 454 (b) of the Implementing Rules and Regulations of RA No. 7160.
observations.managementAction: For CY 2021, the Municipality utilized the 20% for various projects for priority projects mentioned. We likewise recommended the changes in the Annual Budget be implemented in accordance with Article 417 and 454 (b) of the Implementing Rules and Regulations of RA No. 7160.
observations.reason: For CY 2021, realignment of funds was still noted and one project was charged to 20% Development Fund without the proper appropriation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: • submit a letter-inquiry to the Land Registration Authority on the titling of undocumented municipal lands in preparation for their titling; • instruct the Municipal Engineer, MGSO and/or the Municipal Assessor to submit documents regarding actual use of landholdings and estimate/s on the costs regarding the survey and submission of related application titling with the requisite certification/s; • instruct the Municipal Budget Officer to include in the annual budget the cost/s for the titling of landholdings; • direct the Municipal Accountant to record the remaining 29 parcels of land using the available assessed/market value with proper disclosure in the Notes to Financial Statements (NFS), and to record the adjustment of already-recognized land to its current fair market value, with the corresponding disclosures in the NTFS, to afford uniformity in the recognition of Land.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our previous years’ recommendations that the Municipal Mayor
ensure that the Inventory Committee conduct a complete physical inventory of all
the Municipality’s assets and prepare the Report on Physical Count of Property,
Plant and Equipment; devise strategic actions/plans on the conduct of a complete
physical inventory of all Municipal properties; adopt all necessary means to
accomplish the task/plan accordingly. Furthermore, instruct the Municipal
Accountant and the General Services Officer to reconcile their records to address
the reliability and existence of PPE accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct:
a. the Municipal Treasurer to update the Certified List of All Delinquent
Taxpayers and ensure that all Delinquent Accounts were included therein to
come up with a reliable and accurate list of delinquent accounts at year end;
and
b. the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to reconcile their
records to present a reliable RPT and SET Receivables in the financial
statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant
reconcile with the, Municipal Treasurer the ₱5,944.96 unreconciled balance
between the General Ledger and the Cashbook. Likewise, the Municipal
Accountant to recompute the correct balance of Cash in Bank – General fund as
reflected in the bank reconciliation statement and prepare the necessary adjusting
entries to reflect the correct balance of the Cash account and fairly present
municipality’s financial statements as of December 31, 2019.

We reiterated our previous year recommendation that the Municipal Mayor
instruct the Municipal Treasurer effect the cancellation of the stale checks and the
Municipal Accountant make proper adjustments in the municipality’s books of
accounts to fairly present its financial statements as of December 31, 2019.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal
Accountant to coordinate with the Provincial Accountant and reconcile the
balances of the reciprocal accounts Due from/Due to LGUs-Province.

We recommended that Municipal Mayor instruct:
a. the Municipal Treasurer, in coordination with the Market Specialist and the
BPLO, to require stallholders to obtain their business permit;
b. the BPLO to strategize the tightening of the enforcement of the Local Revenue
Code to ensure that all businesses operating within the Municipality have
secured their business permit; and
c. the Municipal Treasurer, in coordination with the BPLO, to recompute the
unpaid taxes of the business-taxpayers corresponding to the understatement
of gross receipts, proceed with the collection thereof, and submit to the Audit
Team the results of the actions taken.
Likewise, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the Sanggunian Bayan
exercise the prerogative to revise and update the Local Revenue Code (LRC)
pursuant to Sections 129 and 191 of RA 7160 to meet the financial needs of the
LGU.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to
set-up and maintain Special Accounts in the General Fund for the monitoring of
operating performance and viability of the Municipality’s economic enterprises
and 20% Development Fund. Moreover, record all transactions pertaining to these
economic enterprises to the corresponding subsidiary ledgers.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor strictly instruct the Municipal
Agriculturist and Municipal Engineer to:
a. plan and execute the recommendations enumerated therein by the DENR-
Mines and Geosciences Bureau;
b. apply and secure the Environmental Compliance Certificate for the
Municipality’s Sanitary Landfill Category 1 Disposal Facility; and
c. accomplish the activities as stated in the 10-Year Solid Waste Management
Plan.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. require the Accounting Office to analyze the accounts with old balances that
remained unreconciled/unadjusted over the years and prepare necessary
adjusting entries to correct the account balance, if any; and
b. require the responsible officials/employees to remit immediately in full the
withheld funds to BIR, GSIS, Philhealth, and Pag-IBIG.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the concerned officials and
employees to require the submission of duly filled-up driver’s trip tickets to
support the payment of gasoline, oil and lubricants totaling ₱4,139,445.63.

Likewise, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the concerned
departments to properly control and account for their gasoline consumption and
use of motor vehicles as provided in the COA Circular No. 77-61 dated September
26, 1977, which shall include:
a. Use of serially numbered Driver’s Trip Ticket which should be properly
accomplished, i.e. part B, which should be filled up by the driver, is complete;
b. Prepare and submit on a monthly basis the Monthly Report of Fuel
Consumption of Motor Vehicles which shall contain data as to the type of
vehicle, plate number and vehicle’s total distance travelled, total fuel used,
distance travelled per litre, normal travel km. per litre and the total litres
consumed used during the reporting month; and
c. Require each driver of the municipality to prepare, at the end of every month, a
Monthly Report of Official Travels summarizing in a chronological order his	rips for the month. The report shall be certified by the driver and approved by
the Department Head.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor to instruct the concerned officials and
employees to:
a. submit copy of purchase orders and all forms of contract together with its
supporting documents to COA within five (5) working days after their
perfection and/or approval as provided under COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated
February 12, 2009;
b. submit Request of Inspection to provide notice of delivery as well as the
Acceptance and Inspection Reports to COA within 24 hours from
receipt/acceptance of goods/items, for the timely review and inspection of the
projects/deliveries;
c. ensure that the POs contain, among others, information as to the date of
delivery, procurement terms and date of conforme of supplier, contact
numbers and complete address of supplier as required under COA Circular No.
96-010 dated August 15, 1996; and
d. strictly comply with Section 4(6) of PD 1445. Ensure that all claims against
government funds be supported with all the required documentations. Require
submission of lacking documents. Refrain from paying disbursement
vouchers with incomplete supporting documents to avoid suspensions and
disallowances in audit.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor instruct the Bids and Awards Committee to
strictly and meticulously follow the procurement process and submit the lacking
documentary requirements enumerated herein.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Local School Board to
submit the School Improvement Plan and the Annual Implementation Plan and
refrain from approving the Special Education Fund Annual Budget without these
supporting documents to ensure that the Special Education Fund (SEF) will be
utilized for the intended purpose or according to the identified priority areas listed
in the Annual Implementation Plan (AIP) culled from the DepEd-approved School
Improvement Plan (SIP).

Likewise, we recommended that the Municipality refrain from charging non-
priority expenses from the Special Education Fund and strictly comply with the
provisions of the above-stated regulations, otherwise, such transactions will be
suspended or disallowed in audit if repeatedly incurred.

Also, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor discontinue the practice of
granting cash advances to DepEd teachers and instead designate a permanent
employee of the Municipality as Special Disbursing Officer to perform disbursing
functions out of the Special Education Fund and henceforth strictly adhere to the
provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 on the grant and utilization of cash
advances.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive, as the chairperson of the Local
Councils for the Protection of Children (LCPC), enjoin the members of the Council
to adhere to the provisions of Rule 15.b of Rules and Regulations Implementing
Republic Act No. 9344 or the "Juvenile Justice and Welfare Act of 2006" and to
DILG Memorandum Circular 2012-120 that mandate the LGU to strengthen and
implement the programs, projects and activities of the Local Councils for the
Protection of Children (LCPC).

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the concerned officials and
employees to allocate funds for the initialization of the Community-Based
Monitoring System (CBMS), and eventually spearhead the establishment of the
GAD database, manual or electronic, consisting of sex-disaggregated data and
other GAD-related information that shall serve as the basis for the formulation of
gender-responsive and gender-sensitive plans and programs in accordance with
the GAD mandate.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Local Development
Council:
a. to ensure full utilization of the 20% Development Fund for the efficient and
effective attainment of desired socio-economic development and
environmental management outcomes;
b. to prioritize the implementation and budgeting of development projects
contained in the Annual Investment Plan pursuant to Sections 106 and 305(i) of
RA 7160 and Article 454(d) of Act Implementing Rules and Regulations; and
c. to submit the Supplemental Annual Investment Plan as approve by the
Sanggunian for the revisions in the 20% Development Plan else justify the
implementation of 12 PPAs not included in the AIP and 5 PPAs which
exceeded their allocated budget.

Likewise, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal
Engineer to publicize the projects/programs/activities in conspicuous places
within the agency premises and in the project venue or notify the public on its
essential features/elements, in compliance with the provisions of COA Circular
No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2014.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, in coordination with the Municipal
Agriculture Office, instruct the Barangay Officials to prioritize funding for the
establishment of Material Recovery Facilities in their respective Barangays, in
compliance with Section 1, Rule XI of the IRR of RA No. 9003 and DILG
Memorandum Circular No. 2009-168, dated October 27, 2009.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the LDRMMO to include in the
LDRRMFIP the PPAs to be funded from the Special Trust Fund after thorough
review, in compliance with the provisions under COA Circular No. 2012-002 and
submit the Sanggunian Panlalawigan/Bayan Resolution and the declaration of
state of calamity relative to the above purchase to avoid disallowance in audit.

We recommended that the:
 The LGU create Office of the GSO, if possible, and also reinstitute its Inventory Committee to conduct complete inventory taking of all the Municipal properties particularly on those not included in the last year, prepare the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) and submit to the Municipal Accountant and Auditor concerned not later than January 31 of every year;
 Make strategic actions/plan on how to conduct a complete physical inventory of all Municipal properties, engage and adopt all the necessary means to accomplish the task/plan accordingly.; and
 The General Services Officer - designate and the Municipal Accountant exert more effort to reconcile both the Inventory Report and Property records. Secure and accomplish copies of Acknowledgement Receipt of Equipment or Property Acknowledgement Receipt of all properties issued to current and former employees in order to hold them accountable; and
 The Municipal Accountant and the GSO-Designate maintain complete and updated PPE Ledger Cards and Property Cards, respectively, to facilitate the reconciliation of both records.
observations.managementAction:
Partially Implemented
The General Services Officer- designate and the Municipal Accountant maintain for current acquisitions only.
observations.reason: The Municipality has not implemented the undertaking. The Accounting Office did not have the manpower for the undertaking. The municipal accountant and the GSO-designate were maintained for current acquisitions only.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to draw the Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to take up the unrecognized properties and completed infrastructure project in the books and its corresponding depreciation to reflect fairly the Municipality’s Property, Plant and Equipment and Government Equity.
observations.managementAction:
Not Implemented
The municipal accountant has not yet drawn the JEV to book up cost.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor create a group headed by the Human Resource Officer to undertake a thorough study and evaluation on the manpower requirements in each department/office to avoid unnecessary jobs and minimize government spending.
observations.managementAction:
Not Implemented
The Municipality has not yet undertaken the study and evaluation of its manpower requirements in connection with labor outsourcing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to exert greater efforts in locating documents that would enlighten on the outstanding cash advances of ₱29,712.85 to allow the formulation and implementation of appropriate action on their liquidation/disposition. We also reminded the Municipal Mayor to instruct the Municipal Accountant to be more vigilant in the monitoring, evaluation and implementation of appropriate action on outstanding cash advances.
observations.managementAction:
Not Implemented
The accounting office has exerted maximum effort to locate the documents but records were not found.

observations.recommendation:
 The Inventory Committee headed by the GSO prepare inventory list by category and
perform the actual and complete physical count of PPE in order to determine the
existence of the recorded PPE accounts and reconcile the results of the count with
the accounting records and establish the causes of discrepancies, whether items
have already been missing, donated, sold and unserviceable to arrive at the correct
balance in the financial statements. As to the infrastructure assets, coordinate with
the Municipal Engineer to verify their existence, update and maintain the required
Property Cards and update ARE to facilitate the
reconciliation of RPCPPE with the accounting records;
 The Accounting Department exhaust all efforts to fully identify the PPEs included in
the beginning/unidentified balances. Secure the necessary documents to support
those PPEs to be able to reconcile with the Inventory Report of the GSO. Likewise,
review the composition of the accounts under those PPEs. Secure proper
documentation before effecting the necessary adjusting entries for erroneous
recording or for inclusion to ensure that the amounts are valid and conform to the
rules and regulations that will cover such adjustments; and
 The Municipal Accountant and the GSO-designate be provided with support of
manpower to be able to prepare complete and updated PPE Ledger Cards, and
Property Cards, respectively, to facilitate the reconciliation of both records.

 The Municipal GSO conduct the physical count of inventories every semester and
prepare the Report of Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI). Submit copy of RPCI to
the Municipal Accountant and to the Audit Team within the prescribed period; and
 The Municipal Accountant reconcile with the submitted RPCI of the GSO with the
records of accounting. Also, apply consistently the perpetual inventory
accounting method on all supplies and materials to fairly present the inventory
and expense accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the following:
 Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to reconcile their records to
present a reliable RPT and SET Receivables in the financial statements; and
 Municipal Treasurer to exhaust all efforts and devise strategies to collect all
delinquent real property taxes and encourage the taxpayer to pay their dues on
time. Conduct verification on those outstanding balances to determine the
accuracy of the receivables and its collectability.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and her
staff to:
(a) Coordinate again with the Provincial Accountant and reconcile their records on
receivables/payables and their corresponding payables/receivables in the books of
the Province to establish the correct balances of the intra-agency accounts; and
(b) Review the composition of the accounts under the Due to LGUs and identify the
appropriate classification for those items identified in the audit. Secure proper
documentation before effecting the necessary adjusting entries for erroneous
recording/inclusion in the Due to LGUs account to ensure that the amounts are valid
and conform to the rules and regulations that will cover such adjustments.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant reconcile with the, Municipal
Treasurer the ₱1,717.95 unreconciled balance between the General Ledger and the
Cashbook and prepare the necessary adjusting entries to reflect the correct balance
of the Cash-Local Treasury account and fairly present municipality’s financial
statements as of December 31, 2018.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer effect the cancellation of the stale
checks and the Municipal Accountant make proper adjustments in the municipality’s
books of accounts to fairly present its financial statements as of December 31, 2018.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Bids and Awards Committee to
strictly observe the provisions of the IRR of RA 9184.

We also recommended for a written justification from the BAC Chairman for the noted
deficiencies and inconsistencies in the procurement process as a) non-conduct of pre-
bid conference, b) non-submission of contracts for review and evaluation by the
Commission on Audit c) failure to provide proof of invitation to bid as observers to a
private group in a sector or discipline and d) non-submission of bid data sheet and
detailed estimates of contractors and award of contract to bidders in spite of the
deficiencies in the bidding process.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and other concerned municipal officials
ensure effective utilization of the 20% Development Fund which is primarily intended to
fund projects that shall contribute to the attainment of desirable socio-economic
development and environmental management outcomes. Any delay in the completion
of development projects means to slow down community development or realization of
development objectives. We also recommended that the development
projects/activities shall be shown in itemized appropriations in the municipal annual
investment program and municipal budget and any changes shall be made pursuant
to Sections 321 and 336 of Republic Act No. 7160 and Articles 417 and 454(b) of the
Act Implementing Rules and Regulations. We further recommend that Annual
Investment Program be filled out according to the existing guidelines to provide an
accurate and useful data.

Based on the forgoing observations, we recommend the Municipal Mayor:
 Require the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Office (MDRRM)
enforce the implementation of the programs and projects of the Municipality
under 70% Mitigation Fund and have those programs completed within the
intended time to avoid delay in the delivery of services and benefits to the
constituents;
 Require the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Office (MDRRM)
to ensure that that the future programs and activities charged to the 5% LDRRMF
are aligned with the Disaster Prevention and Preparedness goals of the Municipality.
Ensure that program and projects are aligned with their intended appropriation; and
 Require LDRRM Officer to submit the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF
on or before the 15th day after the end of each month through LDRRMC and Local
Development Council (LDC) to the COA auditor pursuant to COA Circular 2012-002.

For the foregoing deficiencies, we recommended the following:
 That the Local School Board strictly comply with the provisions of Joint Circular
No. 1, series of 2017 of the Department of Education, Department of Budget and
Management and Department of the Interior and Local Government in order to
ensure that Special Education Fund (SEF) will be utilized for the intended purpose
or according to the identified priority areas listed in the Annual Implementation
Plan (AIP) culled from the DepEd-approved School Improvement Plan (SIP);
 That the Local Chief Executive, as the Head of the Local School Board, adhere
strictly with the existing laws, rules and regulations in the prioritization and
utilization of Special Education Fund particularly for the operation and
maintenance of public schools, construction and repair of school buildings,
facilities and equipment, educational research, purchase of books and
periodicals and sports development as provided in Sections 100(c) and 272 of the
Local Government Code of 1991 and DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum
Circular No. 1, s. 2017 in order to ensure an effective educational development
within the locality;
 Require the concerned Department of Education officials to immediately submit the
above mentioned supporting documents to substantiate the transaction; and
 That the Municipal Accountant ensure that the supporting documents were
complete before the processing of claims pursuant to Section 4 (6) of Presidential
Decree No. 1445 to avoid suspension in audit.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to
make representation with the GSIS to confirm and check the list with existing
balances of unpaid premiums and in-default loan accounts of those identified
personnel and if validated, immediately require concerned individuals to settle said
obligations.

We recommended that the Municipality strictly implement the Safe Closure and
Rehabilitation Plan (SCRP) in accordance with its detailed plan and corresponding
cost estimate that was duly acknowledged by the Environment and Management
Bureau (EMB), CALABARZON Region to insure that the controlled dumpsite closure
and rehabilitation are done in a proper manner, with environmental protection
measures and safeguards in place. We also recommended making the mini sanitary
landfill operational immediately to address the problem of final disposal site for the
municipality’s solid waste. We further recommend that the idle material recovery
facility equipment be utilized as it was intended to help improve solid wastes
reduction efforts.

We recommended that the LGU strengthen, and sustain the Municipal Council for the
Protection of Children (MCPC), pursuant to Section 15 of Republic Act No. 9344 or the
“Juvenile Justice and Welfare Act of 2006.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal
Assessor comply with the documentary requirements to support the payment of lot as
prescribed under P.D. 1445 and COA Circular 2012-001 to ascertain the claim of
ownership.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the department head/s or personnel
concerned to comply with the completion and submission to the Audit Team of the
Agency Action Plan and Status of Implementation (AAPSI) for part III of CY 2018 AAR
and on every AAR thereafter.

We recommended that the Municipality strictly observe the provisions of Sections
321 and 336 of Republic Act No. 7160 and Article 417 454(b)of the Act Implementing
Rules and Regulations and promote the Code objectives of discouraging careless
preparation of the annual budget or any practice that undermines the integration of
planning and budgeting at the local level.
