We also recommended making the mini sanitary landfill operational immediately to address the problem of final disposal site for the municipality’s solid waste. We further recommend that the idle material recovery facility equipment be utilized as it was intended to help improve solid wastes reduction efforts.
observations.managementAction:
Partially Implemented
The management had commented that they are on the process of completing the construction of the sanitary landfill as well as making the material recovery facility operational. As to the garbage collected, particularly the residual wastes they had commented that those wastes undergo soiling and apply solution to control the gases. As to the biodegradable materials, they were used as part of compost.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LGU strengthen, and sustain the Municipal Council for the Protection of Children (MCPC), pursuant to Section 15 of Republic Act No. 9344 or the “Juvenile Justice and Welfare Act of 2006.
observations.managementAction:
Partially Implemented
The Municipal Budget Officer had commented that they included the 1% LCPC Fund in their annual budget. However, the activities therein are social services. Likewise, they noted the audit recommendation and see to it that they will be monitored in the ensuing years

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Assessor comply with the documentary requirements to support the payment of lot as prescribed under P.D. 1445 and COA Circular 2012-001 to ascertain the claim of ownership.
observations.managementAction:
Partially Implemented
The Municipal Accountant had commented that she will coordinate with the Municipal Assessor and have those documents submitted to the audit team once they had been secured.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the department head/s or personnel concerned to comply with the completion and submission to the Audit Team of the Agency Action Plan and Status of Implementation (AAPSI) for part III of CY 2018 AAR and on every AAR thereafter.
observations.managementAction:
Partially Implemented
The management had ensured the completion of the AAPSI within the prescribed period for submission to the audit team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality strictly observe the provisions of Sections 321 and 336 of Republic Act No. 7160 and Article 417 454(b)of the Act Implementing Rules and Regulations and promote the Code objectives of discouraging careless preparation of the annual budget or any practice that undermines the integration of planning and budgeting at the local level.
observations.managementAction:
Partially Implemented
The management had commented that the re-alignment was due to the small budget of the Municipality. However, they noted the audit recommendation and see to it that annual budget changes will be done through supplemental budget.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to review in detail all entries made on the Due from/to Other Funds particularly those made in the General Fund and Trust Fund and check any unrecorded transaction/s in its reciprocal account/s. Draw the Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to make necessary adjustments to present the correct balance of the accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction:
Partially Implemented
The Accounting office prepared books of accounts for special accounts only and for Market and Slaughter House only and did not include the Tuy Public Transport Terminal and 20% Development Fund.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the GAD Focal Point person in coordination with the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator, in coordination with the Municipal Budget Officer: make proper planning for projects under 20% DF and ensure they are expended only for specific and prioritized projects listed in the Approved Annual Investment Plan; closely monitor the usage of the 20% charged to the Development Fund to maximize the usage; ensure that the 20% charged to the Development Fund be expended only for specified development projects; ensure that only those expenditures/PPAs in the approved plan and budget with identified gender issue be funded and implemented out of GAD fund.
observations.managementAction:
Partially Implemented
The GAD Focal Point person in coordination with the Municipal Budget Officer: followed strictly the guidelines prescribed by DBM-NEDA-NCRFW Joint Circular No. 2004-01 in the preparation and execution of the Annual Gender and Development Plan and Budget. Likewise, see to it that the GAD Plan and Budget will be reviewed and approved by the DILG Regional Office and ensure that only those expenditures/PPAs in the approved plan and budget with identified gender issue be funded and implemented out of GAD Plan.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipality to implement the procurement guidelines in Section 7 of the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of Republic Act No. 9184. Ensure that only goods, infrastructure projects, and consulting services which have been included in the approved APP shall be procured thereby precluding occasions for unnecessary government purchases and circumventions of the prescribed procurement procedures such as advance deliveries, ”suki system”, or unwarranted resort to alternative methods of procurement, among others.
observations.managementAction:
Partially Implemented
The municipal employees complied with the procurement services but instances of lapses in bidding process were still observed in CY 2018.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor cause the General Services Office and the Municipal Accountant to comply with the maintenance of subsidiary ledgers and complete set of financial statements of each special account particularly on the operation of the market and slaughterhouse, Tuy Public Transport Terminal and 20% Development Fund to help Management in its decision making.
observations.managementAction:
Partially Implemented
The Accounting Office prepared books of accounts for special accounts for the operation of the market and slaughterhouse only and did not include other special funds like Tuy Public Transport Terminal and 20% Development Fund.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and heads of project concerned offices to prepare and submit the liquidation report to the source agency of fully implemented BUB projects and the Municipal Treasurer prepare the corresponding checks for the return the unutilized portion of the said funds.
observations.managementAction:
Partially Implemented
The unutilized funds were addressed and the concerned documents were being prepared.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the: a. General Services Officer, BAC Chairman, Budget Officer coordinate, and strictly comply with RA 9184 on procurement of supplies and materials utilized for the repairs of government properties be included in the Annual Procurement Plan; b. Municipal Accountant submit the lacking documents and correct deficient supporting documents and stop processing claims with incomplete/deficient documentation; and c. GSO and BAC consolidate requests for supplies and materials of all offices/departments and teachers and ensure that procurement be done through public bidding instead of negotiated-small value procurements.
observations.managementAction:
Partially Implemented
The GSO and BAC coordinated the procurement and some corrective actions were undertaken.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management limit grant of PEI to ₱5,000 as prescribed in DBM Circular No. 2014-003 dated December 2, 2014. We also recommended that all officials and employees refund the excess amount of ₱5,000 each for the PEI received in CY 2014.
observations.managementAction:
Partially Implemented
The disallowed transaction was appealed to the COA Regional Office No. IV-A.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the General Services Officer, BAC Chairman, Municipal Accountant: a) Enjoin the submission of authority from the Office of the Secretary of Department of the Interior and Government (DILG); b) Submit the supporting documents required in National Budget Circular No. 446-A for the procurement of 2nd hand Motor Vehicle for further evaluation; and c) Stop processing claims with incomplete/deficient supporting documents.
observations.managementAction:
Partially Implemented
The Management justified that said authority is moot and academic. Documentary requirements were being coordinated for submission.

observations.recommendation: We recommended refund of the total amount of P1,297,000.00 expended for the grant of AICS to all employees, elected officials and Job Order personnel.
observations.managementAction:
Fully Implemented
The municipal employees complied and refunded the disallowed amount.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the: the Municipal Mayor exercise prudence in the disbursement of government funds. Instruct the Bids and Awards Committee Chairman to comply with the provisions of RA 9184 in undertaking procurement. Require the Municipal Accountant to ensure that withholding taxes on government payments are correctly computed/deducted and promptly remitted to the BIR. Submit the required supporting documents and/or justifications why the disbursement should be allowed in audit.
observations.managementAction:
Fully Implemented
The correct withheld tax amount was remitted to the BIR.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor exercise prudence in the disbursement of government funds. Ensure that the PDAF are spent accordingly to its intended purpose. Strictly comply with the provisions of RA 9184 and COA Circular 2013-004. Submit written guidelines/criteria for the selection of grantees and the amount of the funds unutilized for other purpose.
observations.managementAction:
Fully Implemented
The municipal management provided justification that the purchase of school bags and notebooks was distributed to indigent school children as per sponsored program. The monitoring committee was created.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: submit a letter-inquiry to the Land Registration Authority on the titling of undocumented municipal lands in preparation for their titling; instruct the concerned officials to unearth documents establishing the Municipality's rights/ownership over its landholdings; direct the Municipal Engineer, MGSO and/or the Municipal Assessor to submit documents regarding actual use of landholdings and estimate/s on the costs regarding the survey and submission of related application titling with the requisite certification/s; instruct the Municipal Budget Officer to include in the annual budget the cost/s for the titling of landholdings; and direct the Municipal Accountant to record the remaining 29 parcels of land using the available assessed/market value with proper disclosure in the Notes to Financial Statements (NFS), and to record the adjustment of already-recognized land to its current fair market value, with the corresponding disclosures in the NFS, to afford uniformity in the recognition of Land.
observations.managementAction:
Partially Implemented
Some parcels of land were already excluded in the list, since some of them are properties of the Department of Education, PNP and the barangay. The Municipal budget officer to include cost for titling was constrained by lack of funds. The required disclosure in the Notes to Financial Statements was not made.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the:
• The LGU create Office of the GSO, if possible, and also reinstitute its
Inventory Committee to conduct complete inventory taking of all the
Municipal properties particularly on those not included in the last year,
prepare the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and
Equipment (RPCPPE) and submit to the Municipal Accountant and
Auditor concerned not later than January 31 of every year;
• Make strategic actions/plan on how to conduct a complete physical
inventory of all Municipal properties, engage and adopt all the necessary
means to accomplish the task/plan accordingly;
• The General Services Officer – designate and the Municipal Accountant
exert more effort to reconcile both the Inventory Report and Property
records. Secure and accomplish copies of Acknowledgement Receipt of
Equipment or Property Acknowledgement Receipt of all properties issued
to current and former employees in order to hold them accountable; and
• The Municipal Accountant and the GSO-Designate maintain complete and
updated PPE Ledger Cards and Property Cards, respectively, to facilitate
the reconciliation of both records.

We recommended that:
• Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer reconcile their records
to present an accurate and reliable RPT and SET Receivables in the
financial statements;
• the Municipal Accountant reconcile and ensure the proper recording of
transactions affecting the balances of the RPT Receivable and SET
Receivable which were supposedly of the same amount since each
corresponds to 1% of the assessed value of real property;
• The Municipal Treasurer copy furnish the Sangguniang Bayan with the
Report of Delinquency together with the statement of the reason or reasons
for such non-collection or non-payment for its appropriate action; and
• The Municipal Mayor adopt measures to collect from delinquent taxpayers.
Strengthen and intensify collection system and tax campaign to minimize tax
delinquencies.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant reconcile the municipal
records with the Provincial Accountant and settle discrepancies between the
balances of reciprocal accounts so that the Municipality will be able to present the
correct balances of inter-agency receivables and payables in the financial
statements.

Likewise, that the Municipal Accountant request from the Provincial
Accountant the copies of the JEVs showing all the transactions recorded in the
books of accounts of the Province that should be taken up in the Municipality’s
books of accounts.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Planning and
Development Coordinator, in coordination with the Municipal Budget Officer:
• make proper planning for projects under 20%DF and ascertain that the
name, description, location and estimated cost of each project/activity per
category/program and not on lump sum amount, be indicated in the
Annual Budget and Annual Investment Plan;
• closely monitor the utilization and implementation of priority projects under
the 20% Development Fund to maximize the usage of the Fund; and
• ensure that the 20% Development Fund be expended only for specific and
prioritized projects listed in the Approved Annual Investment Program; and
refrain from charging non-capital expenditures against the 20% Development
Fund.
Likewise, we also recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the
Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting entries to reflect the
proper balances of accounts. Instruct the Municipal Engineer to observe and
implement the standard format for public notification of project/program/activities.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
• Direct the MENRO- Designate and Municipal Engineer to implement the
Safe Closure and Rehabilitation Plan in accordance with the Memorandum
of Agreement with the Department of Environment and Natural Resources-
Environmental Management Bureau (DENR-EMB);
• Strictly enforce the Municipal Ordinance No. 007 series of 2014 “An
Ordinance Adopting the prohibition of Using Plastic Bags on Dry Goods and
Regulating Its Utilization on Wet Goods in the Municipality of Tuy”;
• Direct the MENRO-Designate to see to it that the equipment/mechanisms
installed in the Materials Recovery Facility in the Dumpsite will be utilized in
accordance to its purpose; and
• Direct the MENRO-Designate to exert more efforts for the approval of the
10-year Solid Waste Management Plan and its implementation.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct:
• The MDRRMO to prepare the Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of
LDRRMF certified correct by the Municipal Accountant and submit on or
before the 15th day after the end of each month through the LDRRMC and
Local Development Council.
• The Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary journal entries and
effect the reversion to the General Fund the overstated amount transferred
to the Trust Fund Book amounting to ₱883,127.31.
• The Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer to effect the
necessary journal entries in the accounting books and Registry of
Appropriation, Allotment and Obligations, respectively, for the reversion of
LDRRMF amounting to ₱1,116,799.87 lodged in the trust fund books which
remained unutilized for more than five years.
• The Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting entries for
proper booking of capital expenditures amounting to ₱1,064,525.45.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the members of the
Local School Board to utilize fully the Special Education Fund for the benefit of the
students and formulate a three-year SEF program considering the
programs/projects enumerated in Section 272 of Republic Act 7160 as implemented
by DepEd, DBM and DILG Joint Circulars No. 01 s. 2017 dated January 19, 2017.
Likewise, submit accomplishment report on the finished project of various schools
duly signed by the concerned officials.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
• Instruct the Municipal Engineer to enforce rectification on the project –
Concreting of Putol-Dao road funded under ADM. Submit documents to
support the action taken;
• Create a Municipal Project Monitoring Committee (MPMC) with technical
knowledge, experience or expertise on projects/activities; and
• Require the Municipal Engineer in coordination with the Municipal
Planning and Development Coordinator and MPMC to monitor strictly and
supervise ADM projects and other nationally and locally public work
projects of the LGU, to achieve maximum quality of the finished projects to
the benefit of the government and the general public.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Bids and Awards
Committee (BAC) and Technical Working Group (TWG):
• to scrutinize the sufficiency of the winning bidder’s manpower on all the
projects to be awarded and not on an individual project only in cases where
the latter is the lowest bidder in several projects which have similar or
overlapping implementation period. This is to oblige the winning contractor
to supplement its manpower to be utilized in the projects awarded to it or to
disqualify it from obtaining several awards or contracts in conformity with
the provisions of Section 34.1 and 34.3 of the Revised IRR of RA 9184;
• to monitor contractors with on-going projects with other government
agencies and to coordinate with them involved in implementing
infrastructure projects not disclosed in the Statement of On-going and
Completed projects to avoid overlapping and delay in project
accomplishment and/or substandard quality of work; and
• to conduct the required processes and procedures in all the stages of the
bidding in the procurement of infrastructure and comply strictly with all the
61
documentary requirements as mandated by RA 9184. Non-compliance of the
required documents by the prospective bidder shall be a cause for automatic
disqualification in the eligibility and award of contract.
We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal
Engineer to strictly and closely monitor all projects simultaneously implemented by
the contractors and prepare an individual status report per project to be submitted
to the Honorable Mayor for monitoring purposes and in order to check, verify and
evaluate the quality of work and efficiency of the said contractors.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Engineer
to:
• submit the suspension/extension order issued to contractors and if failed to
do so, submit copy of Statement of Work Accomplished for unperformed
portion as of the target completion date signed by the Municipal Engineer
and duly acknowledged by the contractor on the number of days delayed;
and
• continuously impose strict deduction of Liquidated Damages on delays not
feasibly avoided.

We recommended that the Management:
• Create a team compose of department heads to make a formal study in
improving the revenue of the market and immediately act upon the various
causes of losses;
• Institute actions for the intensive collection of delinquency on monthly rental
of stall fees and impose the sanctions against delinquent stallholders;
• Accept new lessees for the unoccupied/vacant stalls in the market;
• Require the Municipal Treasurer and Market Administrator-Designate to:
o Stop the practice of collecting rentals on a daily basis instead require the
stallholders to pay on a monthly basis and see to it that the corresponding
official receipts will be issued upon payment; and
o Assign ticket supervisor/checker and require them to carry-out their task
for the counter-checking of the issuance of cash tickets by the market
collectors.
• Make a study for possible imposition of fees aside from business permit to
the private-owned business establishments within the vicinity of the public
market who are benefited by the strategic location as an additional source of
income for the market operation;
69
• Provide justification for the awarding of more than 1 stall to a person; and
implement sanctions on stall owners for violating the prohibition against sub-
lease under Section 5A.08e.7 of Revised Revenue Code of 2008;
• Enjoin the Business Permit and Licensing Office and Municipal Treasurer’s
Office in monitoring and exacting permit fee and other regulatory fees for
businesses not only in the market but in all business establishments under its
jurisdiction; and
• Make a reshuffle on the market collectors specially those who did the
collections through cash tickets. Review plantilla positions including job
orders for possible assignments in the operation of market to help in
proper monitoring and control to increase collections.
Moreso, the Municipal Mayor together with the Honorable Members of the
legislative and the concerned officials work together for the strict implementation
of the Revised Revenue Code of 2008 and also for its upgraded revision.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal
Treasurer to strictly comply with the General Appropriations Act (GAA) provision
that monthly take home pay of employee shall not be lower than Four Thousand
Pesos (₱4,000.00).

We recommended that the Municipality (a) reconcile employees’ records at
GSIS, PhilHealth and PAG-IBIG to identify the affected employees on the
unremitted balances and immediately remit the amounts due to these agencies; (b)
identify the cause/s of the over remittance for some accounts and make the proper
adjustments and (c) remit the due GSIS loan amortizations pursuant to Section
14.2, Rule III of the Implementing Rules and Regulations of Government Service
Insurance Act of 1997 or R.A. No. 8291 to avoid the incurrence of unnecessary
financial expenses in the form of imposed penalties.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the honorable members of
the Sanggunian include among its top priorities the construction of a building for
the safekeeping and accommodation of the increasing volume of documents
generated by the various departments/offices of the Municipality, particularly those
for submission to the audit team, with consideration on the preservation period of ten
(10) years as mandated by law.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the following: a. Inventory and Appraisal Committee conduct a complete and actual inventory taking of all the LGU’s properties including the land titles, buildings other structures, equipment and other property, plant and equipment. We recommend further that the GSO and the Municipal Accountant maintain updated property cards and accurate subsidiary ledger for each category of assets, respectively and exert extra efforts in the reconciliation of accounting and property records to reflect the correct balances in the financial statements. b. The GSO adopt the classification of using the Revised Local Government Chart of Accounts. Likewise, the Accounting Office and GSO reconcile their subsidiary records to locate the differences noted. Henceforth, regularly reconcile Property Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards with Property Cards to avoid and readily adjust discrepancies in the Motor Vehicles account. c. The management appropriate in its annual budget the cost of titling of the parcels of land in favor of the Municipality to prevent possible legal dispute in the future.
observations.managementAction:
a. The Local Government Unit of Tuy has always considered aggregating the opportunities it offers specifically on personnel growth and development. This includes the creation of other positions including the General Services Office. However, given the limited financial resources the Local Government Unit possessed, currently, we can attest that the creation of the aforementioned position is still not part of our pipeline. Moreso, tasks and responsibilities of the GSO has been consistently carried out by a General Services Officer-designate who has been, so far, meeting expectations. We wish to assure the Commission that once the LGU has the capability to create the Office, as recommended by the Commission, we will do so as soon as possible.

On the other hand, we wish to inform the Commission that we will prepare a comprehensive report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment and submit such document to the Commission once we have completed it.

b. We would like to inform the Commission that we have been working on several action plans and/or strategies to conduct a more erudite form of physical inventory of all Municipal properties. Once done, we will submit/ inform the Commission of the components of the Plan and strategy and the results we have generated regarding the conduct of the inventory.

c. The General Services Officer-Designate and the Municipal Accountant has been developing a consensus to develop a refined approach in order to reconcile both the Inventory report and Property records. This approach includes the thorough analysis and discussions to integrate the modifications recommended to come up with a vital and substantial report. Furthermore, we will execute and require all employees to secure and accomplish copies of Acknowledgement receipt of equipment or Property Acknowledgement receipt as recommended by the Commission not only to enhance accountability but to ensure proper management and operation of government properties.

d. The Municipal Accountant and the GSO- Designate pledges to maintain a complete and updated PPE ledger cards and Property Cards that will be utilized to reconcile all records involved through the use of a systematic and synchronized mechanism providential for appropriate records management.
observations.reason: Reconciliation of records was not yet done due to conflict of time schedule and heavy workloads.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: a. The Municipal Assessor copy furnish the Municipal Treasurer their monthly assessment report. b. The Municipal Treasurer see to it that the amount of taxes in the certified list of taxpayers being transmitted to the Municipal Accountant tallied with the taxes due counter-checked with the assessment roll of the Municipal Assessor; and c. Municipal Accountant ensure that the set-up of collectibles for each year is supported by a certified list of taxpayers from the Municipal Treasurer’s Office. Likewise, make the necessary adjusting entries for the Special Education Tax Receivables and Deferred Special Education Tax in the Special Education Fund books due to non-conformance to the modified accrual method of accounting for CYs 2015 and 2016 and reconcile its balances with the Real Property Tax Receivables and Deferred Real Property Tax in the General Fund books. Afterwards, the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer reconcile their records and adopt measures to collect from delinquent taxpayers. Strengthen and intensify collection system and tax campaign to minimize tax delinquencies.
observations.managementAction:
a. The Municipal Assessor copy the assessment report; b. The Municipal Treasurer ensure that taxes in the certified list of taxpayers being transmitted to the Municipal Accountant tallied with the taxes due counter-checked with the assessment roll of the Municipal Assessor; c. Municipal Accountant ensure the set-up of collectibles for each year is supported by a certified list of taxpayers from the Municipal Treasurer’s Office. Likewise, make the necessary adjusting entries for the Special Education Tax Receivables and Deferred Special Education Tax in the Special Education Fund books due to non-conformance to the modified accrual method of accounting for CYs 2015 and 2016 and reconcile its balances with the Real Property Tax Receivables and Deferred Real Property Tax in the General Fund books. After reconciliation, the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer will adopt measures to collect from delinquent taxpayers. Strengthen and intensify collection system and tax campaign to minimize tax delinquencies.

The Municipal Assessor is now submitting to the Municipal Treasurer their monthly assessment report; RPT receivables set up for 2017 is supported by a certified list of taxpayers. Likewise, the necessary adjusting entries were made.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to draw a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to take up the unrecorded properties and completed infrastructure project in the books and its corresponding depreciation to reflect fairly the Municipality’s Property, Plant and Equipment and Government Equity.
observations.managementAction: Not Adjusting entries were drawn in CY 2018.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant observe the proper accounting for Trust Funds to fairly present its financial statements and maintain a separate books/subsidiary ledger for each fund. Likewise, the Municipal Treasurer transfer the unutilized funds in the depository bank of the Trust Fund for its proper accounting or cause the refund of the balances of the fully implemented projects to the source agencies.
observations.managementAction: Funds transferred from national government agencies are now directly deposited to the Trust Fund bank account. Likewise, the Municipal Treasurer transferred the unutilized portion for some implemented BUB projects and the corresponding unutilized portion for some fully implemented projects were remitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to review in detail all entries made on the Due from/to Other Funds particularly those made in the General Fund and Trust Fund and check any unrecorded transaction/s in its reciprocal account/s. Draw the Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to make necessary adjustments to present the correct balance of the accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Some adjusting entries were already made.
observations.reason: Still in the process reconciling the reciprocal accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Disbursing Officer utilize the Petty Cash Fund only for small payments for maintenance and operating expenses which cannot be paid conveniently by check or are required to be paid immediately. The Municipal Treasurer to pay the regular expenses thru checks. Ensure that concerned officials and employees submit immediately the required documents for validation. Non-compliance thereof may result to issuance of Notice of Suspension and eventually Notice of Disallowance. Also require refund of the excess reimbursements.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality has stopped payment out of petty cash fund for regular expenses. Likewise, the Municipal Treasurer pay the regular expenses thru checks. Ms. Ma. Michelle D. Ballelos, SPED Teacher refunded the excess reimbursement amounting to ₱300.00.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Disbursing Officer utilize the Petty Cash Fund only for small payments for maintenance and operating expenses which cannot be paid conveniently by check or are required to be paid immediately. Likewise, the Municipal Treasurer pay the regular expenses thru checks.
observations.managementAction: For CY 2017, the Municipal Accountant had stopped payment out of petty cash fund for regular expenses. Likewise, the Municipal Treasurer pay the regular expenses thru checks.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: a) effect the transfer to the Special Trust Fund of a total of ₱1,709,130.94 unexpended LDRRMF from the General Fund; b) disclose the unexpended balance of the LDRRMF in the Notes to Financial Statement thereafter.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant effected the transfer to the Special Trust Fund of a total of ₱1,709,130.94 unexpended LDRRMF from the General Fund. Disclosure of the unexpended balance of the LDRRMF in the Notes to Financial Statement was provided thereafter.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the HRMO to present the issued fingerprint scanners to avoid refund of the equivalent amount if found accountable. Further, utilize the same for its intended purpose as an initial measure of complying with Section 5, Rule XVII of the Omnibus Rules Implementing Book V of Executive Order No. 292 and in order to justify the propriety of payments made for personnel services.
observations.managementAction: The HRMO presented the issued fingerprint scanners in CY 2017 and utilized the same for its intended purpose.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the GAD Focal Point and person in coordination with the Municipal Budget Officer: (a) ensure that only those expenditures/PPAs in the approved plan and budget with identified gender issue be funded and implemented out of GAD fund; (b) ensure that the GAD Plan and Budget will be reviewed and approved by the Provincial and DILG Regional Office and ensure that only those expenditures/PPAs in the approved plan and budget with identified gender issue be funded and implemented out of GAD fund; and (c) see to it that the GAD Plan and Budget will be maintained.
observations.managementAction: For CY 2017, the Management and the Municipal Budget Officer prepared the Annual Gender and Development Plan and Budget. The GAD Plan was reviewed and found compliant by the Provincial Director of the DILG. The Municipality maintained the GAD database. Some implementation of the GAD Fund was used for payment of wages of job orders, gasoline and electricity in the Material Recovery Facility. The management said the GAD Fund will be used henceforth only for projects with identified gender issues.
observations.reason: Some expenditures were still utilized for material recovery facility and wages; full compliance pending.

observations.recommendation: We recommended: a) Strictly implement and effect the immediate rehabilitation of the dumpsite and eventually the construction and maintenance of sanitary landfill in accordance with RA 9003; b) Cause the strict implementation of Municipal Ordinance No. 007 series of 2014; c) Submit to the Audit Team the result of cost-benefit analysis and in-depth research on the use of the Multi-Purpose Shredder Machine Model LMS-400-MPS amounting to ₱1,715,954.00 prior to its procurement; d) Look for possible ways on how the Multi-Purpose Shredder Machine could still be utilized without incurring too much electricity bill and review the warranty provided by the supplier for possible remedies; and e) Expedite and exert more efforts for the approval of the 10-year Solid Waste Management Plan and its implementation.
observations.managementAction: a. The proposed rehabilitation and Safe Closure Plan of the Municipality remains to be unimplemented since we prefer to establish the sanitary landfill first before we utilize the ₱1.4 Million granted by the EMB CALABARZON which is allotted for the rehabilitation and closure of open dump sites. b. The Municipal Ordinance No. 007 has already gained a solid ground when it was implemented in 2014, since we want to replicate best practices of Local Government Units who have been successful in regulating the use of plastic bags in their respective communities. However, a monitoring system has yet to be implemented to safeguard the full realization of the objectives of the Ordinance. A proposal to deploy “Basura Patrollers” who will be tasked to monitor violators of the Ordinance and if possible, impose sanctions to instill discipline and awareness to some extent to every stakeholder of the environmental laws and programs of the Local Government Unit. c. The MENRO-designate has been engaged in evaluating the utilization of every equipment in the Materials Recovery Facility, in fact, the Rapid Composter has been operated every other day to recycle biodegradable wastes recovered from the Public Market to convert such waste into a compost fertilizer. Also, we are trying to touch-base with plastic manufacturers to purchase our shredded plastic waste for the creation of additional raw materials. d. The 10-year Solid Waste Management Plan of the Local Government Unit of Tuy is currently undergoing scrutiny/review from the PG-ENRO. We are exercising tremendous amount of effort to correct and refine the plan before submitting it to the ESWMC for approval.

observations.recommendation:

Inventory and Appraisal Committee conduct a complete and actual inventory taking of all the LGU’s properties including the land titles, buildings other structures, equipment and other property, plant and equipment. We recommend further that the GSO and the Municipal Accountant maintain updated property cards and accurate subsidiary ledger for each category of assets, respectively and exert extra efforts in the reconciliation of accounting and property records to reflect the correct balances in the financial statements.
The GSO adopt the classification of PPEs in the Revised Local Government Chart of Accounts. Likewise, Accounting Office and GSO reconcile their subsidiary records to locate the differences noted. Henceforth, regularly reconcile Property Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards with Property Cards to avoid and readily adjust discrepancies in the Motor Vehicles account.
The management appropriate in its annual budget the cost of titling of the parcels of land in favor of the Municipality to prevent possible legal dispute in the future.

We recommended that:
The Municipal Assessor copy furnish the Municipal Treasurer of its monthly assessment report;
The Municipal Treasurer see to it that the amount of taxes in the certified list of taxpayers being transmitted to the Municipal Accountant tallied with the taxes due counter-checked with the assessment roll of the Municipal Assessor; and
Municipal Accountant ensure that the set-up collectibles for each year is supported by a certified list of taxpayers from the Municipal Treasurer’s Office. Likewise, make the necessary adjusting entries for the Special Education Tax Receivables and Deferred Special Education Tax in the Special Education Fund books due to non-conformance to the modified accrual method of accounting for CYs 2015 and 2016 and reconcile its balances with the Real Property Tax Receivables and Deferred Real Property Tax in the General Fund books.
Afterwards, the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer reconcile their records and adopt measures to collect from delinquent taxpayers. Strengthen and intensify collection system and tax campaign to minimize tax delinquencies.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the  Municipal Accountant to draw Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to take up the unrecorded properties and completed infrastructure project in the books and its corresponding depreciation to reflect  fairly  the Municipality’s Property, Plant and Equipment  and Government Equity.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant observe the proper accounting for Trust Funds to fairly present its financial statements and maintain a separate books/subsidiary ledger for each fund. Likewise, the Municipal Treasurer transfer the unutilized funds in the depository bank of the Trust Fund for its proper accounting or cause the refund of the balances of the fully implemented projects to the source agencies.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to review in detail all entries made on the Due from/to Other Funds particularly those made in the General Fund and Trust Fund and check any unrecorded transaction/s in its reciprocal account/s. Draw the Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to make necessary adjustments to present the correct balance of the accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant strictly comply with the requirement of Section 93 of PD 1445 and the Bids and Awards Committee strictly follow Section 10 of RA 9184 to promote transparency, competitiveness and accountability in the procurement process and impose the necessary taxes due to the national government. Further, require Ms. Ma. Michelle D. Ballelos, SPED Teacher to refund the excess reimbursement amounting to ₱300.00.

We recommended that the Disbursing Officer utilize the Petty Cash Fund only for small payments for maintenance and operating expenses which cannot be paid conveniently by check or are required to be paid immediately. Likewise, the Municipal Treasurer pay the regular expenses thru checks.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer effect the transfer to the Special Trust Fund of a total of ₱1,709,130.94 unexpended LDRRMF from the General Fund.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant provide disclosure of the unexpended balance of the LDRRMF in the Notes to Financial Statement thereafter.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Human Resource Management Officer (HRMO) to present the issued fingerprint scanners  to avoid refund of the equivalent amount totaling ₱39,800.00, if found accountable.  Further, utilize the same for its intended purpose as an initial measure of complying with Section 5, Rule XVII of the Omnibus Rules Implementing Book V of Executive Order No. 292 and in order to justify the propriety of payments made for personnel services.

We recommended that the GAD Focal Point person in coordination with the  Municipal Budget Officer follow strictly the guidelines prescribed by DBM-NEDA-NCRFW Joint Circular No. 2004-01 in the preparation and  execution of the Annual Gender and Development Plan and Budget.  Likewise, see to it that the GAD Plan and Budget will be reviewed and approved by the DILG Regional Office and ensure that only those expenditures/PPAs in the approved plan and budget with identified gender issue be funded and implemented out of GAD fund.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant check the completeness of required documents before approval of the claim. Likewise, the Bids and Awards Committee Chairman provide explanation for the inconsistencies in the different phases of the procurement process and submit all the lacking documents to the Auditor concerned  for further evaluation and to avoid suspension/disallowance in audit.

We recommended that the Municipality duly implement the guidelines in procurement planning specified in Section 7 of the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of Republic Act No. 9184.  Ensure that only goods, infrastructure projects, and consulting services which have been included in the approved APP shall be procured thereby precluding occasions for unnecessary government purchases and circumventions of the prescribed procurement procedures such as advance deliveries, ”suki system”, or unwarranted resort to alternative methods of procurement, among others.

We recommended that the Municipality strictly observe provisions of Article 210 to 213 and 215 of RA 7160 and Section 3.4 of National Budget Circular No. 563 April 22, 2016.  Contributions to leagues which are not included in the preceding articles are not government expenses and should not be charged against the funds of the municipality. In addition, the previously mentioned budget circular specifically disallows individual membership fees paid to private organizations.  Likewise, the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer/Petty Cash Fund Custodian should check the validity of expenses being charged in the Petty Cash Fund and require Mr. Rionel G. Matira to refund the ₱450.00 reimbursed for the renewal of his PRC license.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant strictly comply with Section 313 of RA 7160 and Section 110 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume  I. Maintain and provide complete set of subsidiary ledgers for the preparation of financial statements of each special account particularly on the operation of the market and slaughterhouse, Tuy Public Transport Terminal and 20% Development Fund to help  Management in its  decision making.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator, in  coordination  with the Municipal Budget Officer:     (a) make proper planning with detailed listing of the priority projects and  appropriation  for  individual projects/activity; b) ensure that the 20% Development Fund be expended only for specific and prioritized projects listed in the Approved Annual Investment Program; and (c) closely monitor the utilization and implementation of priority  projects under the 20% Development  Fund  to maximize the usage of the Fund.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and heads of concerned offices to prepare and submit the liquidation report to the source agency of fully implemented BUB projects and the Municipal Treasurer prepare the corresponding checks for the return the unutilized portion of the said funds.

We recommended  that the Municipal Mayor direct the following:
Inventory and Appraisal  Committee conduct a complete and actual inventory taking of all the LGU’s properties  including the land titles, buildings other structures, equipment and other property, plant and equipment. We recommended further that the GSO and the Municipal Accountant maintain updated property cards and accurate subsidiary ledger for each category of assets, respectively and exert extra efforts in the reconciliation of accounting and property records to reflect the correct balances in the financial statements. Also, pursue the titling of parcels of land under the name of the Municipality to prevent possible legal dispute in the future. 

Accounting and Property Units reconcile their subsidiary records to locate the differences noted.  Henceforth, regularly reconcile Property Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards with Property Cards to avoid and readily adjust discrepancies in the Motor Vehicles account.

The General Services Officer file an application for disposal of unserviceable properties together with the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Properties (IIRUP) with the Commission on Audit. Strictly comply with the existing laws and procedures on disposal of unserviceable properties.

Inventory and Appraisal Committee determine and evaluate the carrying value or fair market value of prior years’ acquisition without available records, after which the Municipal Accountant provide for depreciation to reflect the correct carrying amounts of PPE in the financial statements.
